notes:
- Key concepts introduced
  - Termination condition: a mechanism to stop a team/group-chat run based on logical criteria so runs don't go on forever.
  - Built-in termination conditions: provided by a base termination condition class you can inherit from or use directly.
  - Stateful but auto-reset: termination conditions maintain state during a run, but are automatically reset after each run or run_stream; you can also call .reset() manually.
  - Composability: termination conditions can be combined with logical operators (and / or).
  - Max message termination: stops after a specified number of messages produced (counts both agent and task messages; user/task messages count).
  - Text-mention termination: stop when a message contains a specific token/phrase (e.g., a reviewer agent says "terminate" or "approve").
  - Token-usage termination: stop when token usage threshold reached (straightforward mention).
  - Handoff termination: stop when a handoff occurs to a particular agent (useful for workflows like refunds to pause for user-in-the-loop inspection).
  - Source / external termination: stop triggered externally (e.g., a "stop" button in chat UIs).
  - Stop-message / text-message / function-call / function-termination: other event-based termination triggers (e.g., a specific stop message, a function call or function-based termination).
  - Differences between max_turns and max_message: max_turns exists for some team types (e.g., round-robin) but not all group-chat/team types; max_message is broader.
  - Usage pattern: termination conditions are passed into a team/group-chat; they are checked at each run iteration and stop the flow when satisfied.

- Brief explanations / behavior notes
  - Max message counts include both agent and task messages; the initial user/task message counts toward the limit.
  - When combining conditions (e.g., "10 turns OR approve"; or "10 turns AND approve"), the logical combination controls stopping behavior (stop when either or both criteria met depending on OR/AND).
  - After changing termination conditions, you may need to re-create/redefine the team object (or re-run) because conditions are reset per run and team context/state may require redefinition to take effect.

- Analogies or metaphors used
  - Text-mention termination is likened to "that intelligence" in a human team where a reviewer/manager decides work is done.
  - External termination compared to the stop button in ChatGPT-like chat interfaces.
  - "Party" metaphor: when code is approved the speaker will have a party (used to illustrate approval stopping the process).

- Any code, commands, or config shown (paraphrased / captured)
  - import example:
    - from autogen.agent_chat.conditions import max_message_termination  (transcript phrasing: "from autogen agent chat conditions. Yeah import max message termination.")
  - Example usage (described):
    - Define termination = MaxMessageTermination(max=1) or MaxMessageTermination(max=3)
    - Pass termination condition(s) into team/group-chat when creating/running team
  - Example prompt for critique agent:
    - "Provide constructive feedback for every message response with approve when your feedbacks are addressed."
  - Example prompt for review agent:
    - "You have to say approve once the story is complete and you like the full flow. Else provide your feedback."
  - Note: mention of a "function console" utility to print run streams nicely (no explicit API shown).

- Warnings, gotchas, or important caveats
  - Max-message counts include both agent messages and the initial task/user message — this can make the observed message count smaller than expected.
  - max_turns may not be available for non-round-robin/other team types; don't assume all team types support max_turns.
  - Termination conditions are stateful; they auto-reset after run or run_stream. If you run the team again, they start fresh — but if you change termination configs you might need to reinstantiate the team.
  - Forgetting to redefine/recreate the team after changing termination settings can produce unexpected results (transcript example: expected counts were wrong because the team wasn't redefined).
  - When combining conditions, be clear whether you mean AND or OR to avoid unintended early or late stopping.

- Comparisons or lists (potential table candidates)
  - Built-in termination conditions (list):
    - Max message termination: stop after N messages (counts agent + task messages).
    - Text-mention termination: stop when a specific text/keyword is observed (e.g., "approve", "terminate").
    - Token-usage termination: stop when token usage exceeds a threshold.
    - Handoff termination: stop when handoff to a particular agent occurs (useful for manual inspection/refunds).
    - Source / external termination: externally triggered stop (UI stop button).
    - Stop message / text message / function call / function termination: stop on specific message/events/function calls.
  - Properties of termination conditions:
    - Callable (accepts sequence/context)
    - Stateful during run
    - Auto-reset after run/run_stream
    - Combinable via and/or

- Direct quotes worth preserving
  - "A termination condition is a callable..."
  - "They are stateful but reset automatically after each run."
  - "Max message termination stops after a specified number of messages have been produced including both agent and task message."
  - "We can ask one of our agent ... the final reviewer agent to tell us if the task has been done then we can ask it to say terminate and when it will say terminate we will stop our team."
  - "This is kind of that intelligence."
  - "So I can have say a text mention termination. I can either make them run for 10 turns or text mention when one of them say approve."
  - "If you want to reset them effectively you can just dot reset them but after each run or run stream they will get reset automatically."
  - "They can be combined using and and or."
